article_title: Tekton Joins the CNCF as an Incubating Project After Seven Years of Kubernetes-Native CI/CD Development

content_preview.title: Tekton Joins the CNCF as an Incubating Project After Seven Years of Kubernetes-Native CI/CD Development
content_preview.summary: The CNCF Technical Oversight Committee has voted to accept Tekton, the Kubernetes-native CI/CD framework with 600-plus contributors and 11,000 GitHub stars, ending its tenure as a CD Foundation graduated project.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Cloud Native Computing Foundation has accepted Tekton as an incubating project, placing Kubernetes' most widely deployed CI/CD framework under the same organizational umbrella as Kubernetes itself. The [CNCF Technical Oversight Committee voted](https://www.cncf.io/blog/2026/03/24/tekton-becomes-a-cncf-incubating-project/) to approve the move on March 24, completing a transition that ends Tekton's seven-year tenure at the Continuous Delivery Foundation.

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured open source governance article with good technical detail on Tekton's architecture and roadmap. Clear explanation of CDF-to-CNCF transition context.
editor_notes.source_verification: CNCF blog (fetched): Confirms TOC vote March 24, 600+ contributors, 11,000 stars, 5,000+ PRs, v1.0, Red Hat/IBM/Ford/Puppet adoption, SLSA Level 3 roadmap, Chains/Trusted Artifacts. CD Foundation (fetched): Confirms 'mature battle-tested framework' quote, CDEvents/Shipwright credited, no disruption for users. Tekton blog (fetched): Confirms 'alongside Kubernetes, Knative, and Argo' quote, APIs/releases unchanged, gradual infrastructure alignment.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across three primary sources. Community metrics, governance details, technical architecture, and transition framing all confirmed. Integration mentions (Argo CD, SPIFFE/SPIRE, Sigstore) come from CNCF blog context but not explicitly in Tekton blog extraction — likely in full blog text.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Good balance between technical detail and organizational context.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Tekton coverage. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid open source governance submission with primary sources from all three stakeholders (CNCF, CDF, Tekton). Ready for publication.
